title: ForgeBrain Topic Selection Decision
description: The structured decision object produced by the topic selector. Formalizes the decision object described in topic-selector-spec.md Section 6 as a JSON Schema, closing the one gap in brain/ where every other pipeline stage already had a dedicated output schema (research, lesson, content strategy, script, storyboard). Produced by the Curriculum Engine / Topic Memory pairing described in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and consumed by the research stage as its selected_topic input.

mode.description: Which decision mode produced this result. See topic-selector-spec.md Section 4.

selected_topic_id.description: The chosen topic's id, matching an entry in curriculum/java-roadmap.json, or null if no eligible candidate existed. A null selection is a valid, complete answer — see topic-selector-spec.md Section 8.

selected_topic_title.description: The chosen topic's title, for readability. Null exactly when selected_topic_id is null.

reason.description: Plain-language explanation of why this topic won, or why nothing qualified. Must be specific enough to audit the decision without re-running the scoring model.

score.description: The selected topic's final weighted score from topic-ranking.json. Null exactly when selected_topic_id is null.

candidate_topics.description: Every topic that passed all gates for this mode, each with its score and per-factor breakdown.

rejected_topics.description: Every topic considered and excluded, each with a short reason.

blocked_by_prerequisite.description: topic_ids excluded specifically because a prerequisite had not reached posted status, even if the topic was sitting in memory.queue.

blocked_by_recent_use.description: topic_ids excluded specifically because of an active avoid_until cooldown.

needs_revision.description: topic_ids currently at memory status needs_revisit, surfaced for visibility regardless of which mode this run used.

selection_timestamp.description: When this decision was computed. Explicit rather than implicit 'now' so decisions are reproducible.

factors.description: Per-factor values from topic-ranking.json, present even for factors this mode weights at 0.0, for full transparency.
